steps.instruction: A box plot displays: minimum, Q1, median, Q3, and maximum.
steps.explanation: The box spans from Q1 to Q3. The line inside is the median. Whiskers extend to min and max.
The IQR is the width of the box — the spread of the middle 50%.

steps.instruction: Each section (whisker and half-box) contains about 25% of the data.
steps.work: About 25% of data falls in each: below 35, 35-50, 50-65, above 65.
steps.explanation: Even if sections look different widths, each contains roughly 25% of data points.

title: Creating a Box Plot from Data

steps.explanation: Always sort before finding the five-number summary.

steps.instruction: Min = 5, Max = 30, Median (middle) = 15. Lower half: 5,8,10,12 → Q1 = 9. Upper half: 18,22,25,30 → Q3 = 23.5.
Assemble the box plot from the five-number summary: the box spans from Q1 to Q3, a vertical line marks the median inside the box, and whiskers extend from the box to the min and max.
steps.work: Box: 9 to 23.5. Median line at 15. Left whisker to 5. Right whisker to 30.
steps.explanation: The box plot is now complete.
